title: Add restricted project-understanding assistant with versioned evidence records
notes: Created from human suggestion #24 (submitted by mannyrayner on 2026-05-30) and broadened after follow-up discussion. The requested capability is an admin/trusted-user project-understanding assistant that answers high-level questions about publicly available aspects of C-LARA-2 using repository materials as evidence. Initial implementation work began on 2026-05-31 with a core prompt-wrapper/request function and Markdown evidence-record rendering helpers. By 2026-06-01 the architecture had been revised to delegate repository exploration to `codex exec` rather than a normal API/retrieval wrapper, and a first admin-only platform implementation was working: `src/core/project_understanding.py` builds the versioned prompt, invokes `codex exec` in read-only/non-interactive mode, passes `OPENAI_API_KEY` through a reduced environment, resolves common Windows npm Codex shims, captures elapsed time/stdout/stderr/exit status/model/prompt version/token count where extractable from stdout or stderr, computes a conservative output-priced cost estimate when only total Codex tokens are available, parses the final answer, and can render/write versionable records. The Django platform now exposes `/admin-tools/project-understanding/` for staff users, queues runs through Django Q, records `TaskUpdate` progress and heartbeat messages, persists request/result JSON under `MEDIA_ROOT/admin_project_understanding/`, and polls a status endpoint until the answer is available. Manual smoke tests produced plausible cited answers for repository-summary and annotated-text-format questions, demonstrating that Codex can inspect repository files itself. Remaining work includes an explicit export/review flow into `docs/project_understanding/`, reviewer assessment controls, citation/path sanitization for non-admin contexts, hard budget/rate limiting, curated evaluation records for the report, and resolving the existing migration conflict that blocks the admin-tool Django test command in this checkout. Priority remains P2: strategically important for project maintenance and the C-LARA-2 autonomy/authorship evidence case, but restricted until review, cost, prompt-injection, and evidence-quality controls are complete.
